channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Kiro Specs: Deja de Improvisar y Empieza a Planificar Features con IA
item.description: Si alguna vez arrancaste a codear una feature "porque ya sabes lo que hay que hacer" y terminaste reescribiendo todo a mitad del camino... este post es para ti. Kiro Specs es una de las...

item.title: RAG vs MCP is the wrong debate — here's the right framing for production AI systems
item.description: The question I keep seeing in every AI engineering forum right now: "Should we use RAG or MCP?" It's the wrong question. And the fact that it's being asked at all tells me the field hasn't yet...

item.title: I Built a System That Catches Hackers in Real Time
item.description: I Built a System That Catches Hackers in Real Time ​The Problem ​It was a regular Tuesday morning at cloud.ng, a rapidly growing cloud storage company powered by Nextcloud. Thousands of users were...

item.title: Tips for passing CKAD exam at 1st attempt (2026 Edition)
item.description: How it all started Last year we got news: our new client would be using Kubernetes extensively. I was both excited and scared, as I had only limited exposure to Docker in a professional setting,...

item.title: Creating and Configuring an Azure Storage Account for IT Teams
item.description: Whether you are stepping into cloud computing or looking to move from physical storage infrastructure, Azure Storage is one of the best places to start. Managing data on local hardware comes with...

item.title: How to Use Claude 3.5 Sonnet 2026-02 to Generate Tests for Python 3.13 Codebases
item.description: Python 3.13’s 42% faster JIT-compiled hot paths and new type system features make legacy test generation tools obsolete—but Claude 3.5 Sonnet 2026-02 cuts test authoring time by 78% for senior...

item.title: Real-Time Anomaly Detection Engine for a Cloud Storage Platform
item.description: I built a Python daemon that watches incoming HTTP traffic in real time, learns what "normal" looks like, and automatically blocks attackers using Linux's built-in firewall — all without any...

item.title: BP profits more than double as Iran war sends oil prices higher
item.description: Article URL: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c2eveyvgn9no Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47940545 Points: 11 # Comments: 1

item.title: I built Agent Shield an open-source traffic control layer for AI coding agents.
item.description: The idea is simple: AI agents send a lot of stuff over the network: prompts, code snippets, logs, tool output, telemetry, sometimes secrets. Most of that is hidden inside the CLI. Agent Shield...

item.title: The Quiet Revolution at Google Cloud Next '26: Your Database Can Talk to Your AI Agent — No Bridge Required published
item.description: This is a submission for the Google Cloud NEXT Writing Challenge Everyone at Google Cloud Next '26 is talking about Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. The flashy keynote demo, the "era of the agent...

item.title: Elon Musk tells the jury that all he wants to do is save humanity
item.description: On the stand, Elon Musk is positioning himself as a savior. In the high-profile trial between him and his fellow OpenAI co-founder, now CEO, Sam Altman, Musk opened by going through his...

item.title: Taylor Swift is stepping up the legal war on AI copycats
item.description: Taylor Swift has been at the center of AI imitation controversies for years, and now, she's become the latest celebrity who's escalating attempts to protect herself from AI copycats. As usual,...

item.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
item.description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

item.title: Now YouTube TV lets you multiview any channel you want
item.description: YouTube CEO Neal Mohan announced Tuesday that YouTube TV is getting "fully customizable" multiview. Users have "full control to mix and match live streams" and can "build the personalized viewing...

item.title: The FCC is going after the broadcast licenses of Disney-owned ABC stations
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has ordered the ABC stations owned and operated by Disney to file for an early license renewal, as reported earlier by The New York Times. In a filing on...

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: Elon Musk takes the stand in high-profile trial against OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk officially began his testimony in the trial he has brought against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and company president Greg Brockman. The three were on the initial founding team of OpenAI, with...

item.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
item.description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

item.title: You can get dragged into a police investigation by proximity alone — for now
item.description: A years-old bank heist may soon have major privacy implications for every American who owns a cellphone. On Monday, the Supreme Court heard arguments in Chatrie v. United States, a case involving...

item.title: ‘It’s Undignified’: Hundreds of Workers Training Meta’s AI Could Be Laid Off
item.description: More than 700 people working for a Meta contractor in Ireland are at risk of losing their jobs, documents show.

item.title: Google Home makes it easier to understand why devices aren’t working
item.description: Thanks to Google Home's latest update, users may be able to get offline devices working again with less troubleshooting. Now, whenever a device goes offline, users may get "instant...

item.title: The Nex Playground gaming console is $60 off this week
item.description: One of the best-selling gaming consoles over the winter holidays was the Nex Playground, a small camera-equipped cube that runs motion-controlled games made primarily for kids. It got a $50 price...

item.title: Researchers Discover Critical GitHub CVE-2026-3854 RCE Flaw Exploitable via Single Git Push
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security vulnerability impacting GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server that could allow an authenticated user to obtain remote code...

item.title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
item.description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

item.title: The resurrected Commodore 64 is getting a facelift like the original
item.description: The creators of the C64 Ultimate, a recreation of the iconic '80s personal computer that uses an FPGA chip to accurately replicate the original, have announced a follow-up version that continues...

item.title: Brazilian LofyGang Resurfaces After Three Years With Minecraft LofyStealer Campaign
item.description: A cybercrime group of Brazilian origin has resurfaced after more than three years to orchestrate a campaign that targets Minecraft players with a new stealer called LofyStealer (aka GrabBot). "The...

item.title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
item.description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

item.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
item.description: The Google Translate logo sits in the middle of colorful illustrated scenes, including people translating live conversations and a birthday cake topped by candles with the numeral 20

item.title: Introducing NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: Long-Context Multimodal Intelligence for Documents, Audio and Video Agents
VECT 2.0 Ransomware Irreversibly Destroys Files Over 131KB on Windows, Linux, ESXi
item.description: Threat hunters are warning that the cybercriminal operation known as VECT 2.0 acts more like a wiper than a ransomware due to a critical flaw in its encryption implementation across Windows,...
